title: Probability in density curves
description: Practice using area to find probability with density curves.

assessment_items.question_content:
Consider the density curve below. 

![A density curve is plotted on an x y coordinate plane. The curve is a diagonal line segment which increases from (1, 0.25) to (3, 0.75).](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/72d7b755c7e82f78121951f25de825a78778a61b)

**Find the probability that $x$ is less than $2$.**

$P(x < 2)=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
The probability that $x$ is less than $2$ is represented by the proportion of total area under the density curve when $x < 2$:

![The previous density curve is displayed. A horizontal, trapezoidal area under the curve is shaded between 1 and 2 on the x axis. The left, shorter base has a length from 0 to 0.25 on the y axis, and the right, longer base has a length from 0 to 0.50. It has a height from 1 to 2 on the x axis.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/bfeb534405aad181a3337c2788562aa7f7094c46)

We can use the area formula for a trapezoid to find this area:

$\text{Area} = \dfrac{1}{2} (\text{base}_1+\text{base}_2)(\text{height})$

assessment_items.question_content:
Consider the density curve below. 

![A density curve is plotted on an x y coordinate plane. The curve is a diagonal line segment which increases from (1, 0.25) to (3, 0.75).](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/72d7b755c7e82f78121951f25de825a78778a61b)

**Find the probability that $x$ is more than $2$.**

$P(x > 2)=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
The probability that $x$ is more than $2$ is represented by the proportion of total area under the density curve when $x > 2$:

![The previous density curve is displayed. A horizontal, trapezoidal area under the curve is shaded between 2 and 3 on the x axis. The left, shorter base has a length from 0 to 0.50 on the y axis, and the right, longer base has a length from 0 to 0.75. It has a height from 2 to 3 on the x axis.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/27591bc85db7d3334a9e739d6657c8cfac9c6134)

We can use the area formula for a trapezoid to find this area:

$\text{Area} = \dfrac{1}{2} (\text{base}_1+\text{base}_2)(\text{height})$

assessment_items.question_content:
Consider the density curve below. 

![A density curve is plotted on an x y coordinate plane. Two line segments form the density curve. The first segment increases from (3, 0) to (4, 0.4). The other segment extends horizontally from (4, 0.4) to (6, 0.4).](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/20c8d48a8cddd4d5eaa14e6f9119e3547bdab399)

**Find the probability that $x$ is less than $3$.**

$P(x < 3)=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
The probability that $x$ is less than $3$ is represented by the proportion of total area under the density curve when $x < 3$:

![The previous density curve is displayed. No area under the curve is shaded.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/20c8d48a8cddd4d5eaa14e6f9119e3547bdab399)

There is no area under the density curve when $x<3$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Consider the density curve below. 

![A density curve is plotted on an x y coordinate plane. Two line segments form the curve. The first increases from (3, 0) to (4, 0.4). The second extends horizontally from (4, 0.4) to (6, 0.4).](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/20c8d48a8cddd4d5eaa14e6f9119e3547bdab399)

**Find the probability that $x$ is more than $3$.**

$P(x > 3)=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
The probability that $x$ is more than $3$ is represented by the proportion of total area under the density curve when $x > 3$:

![The previous density curve is displayed. A vertical, trapezoidal area under the curve is shaded between 3 and 6 on the x axis. The bottom, longer base has a length from 3 to 6 on the x axis, and the top, shorter base has a length from 4 to 6. It has a height from 0 to 0.4 on the y axis.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/e2170648cf256801e8ef27f38197391ed4d9b147)

That is the entire area. By definition, the area under a density curve is equal to $1$.

We can use the area formula for a trapezoid to verify this area:

$\text{Area} = \dfrac{1}{2} (\text{base}_1+\text{base}_2)(\text{height})$

assessment_items.question_content:
Consider the density curve below. 

![A density curve is plotted on an x y coordinate plane. The curve is a diagonal line segment which decreases from (1, 0.5) to (5, 0).](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/fa84978d3c7247da6051410516e50b2e6892d890)

**Find the probability that $x$ is less than $3$.**

$P(x < 3)=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
The probability that $x$ is less than $3$ is represented by the proportion of total area under the density curve when $x < 3$:

![The previous density curve is displayed. A horizontal, trapezoidal area under the curve is shaded between 1 and 3 on the x axis. The left, longer base has a length from 0 to 0.5 on the y axis, and the right, shorter base has a length from 0 to 0.25. It has a height from 1 to 3 on the x axis.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/9215d6d080ce965f513f69ff2199765abcf9af93)

We can use the area formula for a trapezoid to find this area:

$\text{Area} = \dfrac{1}{2} (\text{base}_1+\text{base}_2)(\text{height})$

assessment_items.question_content:
Consider the density curve below. 

![A density curve is plotted on an x y coordinate plane. The curve is a diagonal line segment which decreases from (1, 0.5) to (5, 0).](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/fa84978d3c7247da6051410516e50b2e6892d890)

**Find the probability that $x$ is more than $3$.**

$P(x > 3)=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
The probability that $x$ is more than $3$ is represented by the proportion of total area under the density curve when $x > 3$:

![The previous density curve is displayed. A triangular area under the curve is shaded between 3 and 5 on the x axis. The triangle has a base from 3 to 5 on the x axis and a height from 0 to 0.25 on the y axis.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/049bfb428f55e575ed2e78d55d9c4f0a49095055)

We can use the area formula for a triangle to find this area:

$\text{Area} = \dfrac{1}{2} (\text{base}) (\text{height})$

assessment_items.question_content:
Consider the density curve below. 

![A density curve is plotted on an x y coordinate plane. Two line segments form the curve. The first increases from (0, 0) to (2, 0.2). The second extends horizontally from (2, 0.2) to (6, 0.2).](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/33f4d87d540520d825cbbb62c40248312e2f7bc3)

**Find the probability that $x$ is more than $2$.**

$P(x > 2)=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
The probability that $x$ is more than $2$ is represented by the proportion of total area under the density curve when $x > 2$:

![The previous density curve is displayed. A rectangular area under the curve is shaded between 2 and 6 on the x axis. The rectangle has a length from 2 to 6 on the x axis and a width from 0 to 0.2 on the y axis.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/e4c41fd5f4f0405382b838af09a1b8c8c713bfed)

We can use the area formula for a rectangle to find this area:

$\text{Area} =  (\text{length}) (\text{width})$

hints:

![The previous density curve is displayed. A triangular area under the curve is shaded between 1 and 2 on the x axis. The triangle has a base from 1 to 2 on the x axis and a height from 0 to 0.2 on the y axis.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/d87448038b183a2b40fe40037641b27a58a07a49)

We can use the area formula for a triangle to find this area:

$\text{Area} = \dfrac{1}{2} (\text{base}) (\text{height})$

assessment_items.question_content:
Consider the density curve below. 

![A density curve is plotted on an x y coordinate plane. The curve is a horizontal line segment which extends horizontally from (1, 0.25) to (5, 0.25).](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/36d1c44340cd235ab42c26016c0b23d4e5bef1da)

hints:

We can use the area formula for a rectangle to find this area:

$\text{Area} =  (\text{length}) (\text{width})$

assessment_items.question_content:
Consider the density curve below. 

![A density curve is plotted on an x y coordinate plane. Two line segments form the curve. The first decreases from (1, 0.4) to (3, 0). The second increases from (3, 0) to (5, 0.6).](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/64e1760972516e6d8d1d40209d95cc2f4da3f124)

**Find the probability that $x$ is less than $3$.**

$P(x < 3)=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
The probability that $x$ is less than $3$ is represented by the proportion of total area under the density curve when $x < 3$:

![The previous density curve is displayed. A triangular area under the curve is shaded between 1 and 3 on the x axis. The triangle has a base from 1 to 3 on the x axis and a height from 0 to 0.04 on the y axis.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/a22740864a5f7ee25af57984f73d79e1f33f328b)

We can use the area formula for a triangle to find this area:

$\text{Area} = \dfrac{1}{2} (\text{base}) (\text{height})$
